Accelerate: ACCELERATOR pedal makes a car speed up
Accumulate: Piles up like a CUMULus cloud growing
Acknowledge: ackKNOWledge — you KNOW and admit it
Adapt: An ADAPTER fits any plug — adjust
Adjacent: A-JAY-cent sits right next to you
Aggregate: Gathers bits into one GREAT heap
Appendix: Added at the end, like the book section

Assemble: Parts joined on an ASSEMBLY line
Attain: AT-TAIN the peak you aimed for

Attribute: You TRIBUTE a trait to its cause
Authority: The AUTHOR of the rules commands
Circumstance: The CIRCle of conditions around an event

Commodity: A COMMODITY is common goods to trade

Complement: COMPLEMENT completes — spelled with an E, it complEtes

Concede: CONCEDE — you 'seed' the point, give in and admit
Concept: A CONCEPT is an idea you conceive

Conclude: CONCLUDE closes it — the end of the road
Concurrent: CON-CURRENT — two currents running at the same time

Confine: CONFINE within a FINE line, restricted
Conflict: CONFLICT — forces FLICK against each other, clash

Confront: CONFRONT face-to-face, at the FRONT of the problem
Conserve: CONSERVE saves — a CONSERVAtory protects plants
Consistent: CONSISTENT stays the same, steady over time
Consolidate: CONSOLIDATE makes SOLID — combine into one strong whole
Construct: CONSTRUCT builds — like a construction site
Consume: CONSUME uses it up — a CONSUMER eats and spends

Contain: CONTAIN holds within — a conTAINer keeps it inside
Contemplate: CONTEMPLATE deeply — like a monk at the TEMPLE
Contemporary: CONTEMPORARY — with the TEMPO of the present time

Contradict: CONTRADICT — CONTRAry words that DICTate the opposite
Contrary: CONTRARY goes CONTRA, against and opposite
Convene: CONVENE — people conVENE, come together to meet

Converge: CONVERGE — lines merge toward one point
Convert: CONVERT changes form — reVERSE into something new
Convey: CONVEY carries — a conVEYor belt moves things along
Cooperate: CO-OPERATE — operate together toward a goal
Coordinate: CO-ORDINATE — order things together in harmony
Correspond: CORRESPOND matches, or you send letters back and forth
Corrupt: CORRUPT — 'core-rupt', the core is broken, dishonest
Criterion: A CRITERION is the standard a CRITIc judges by
Cultivate: CULTIVATE — grow it through CULTUre and care
Cumulative: CUMULATIVE piles up like a CUMULus cloud, adding on
Debate: DEBATE — you BAT ideas back and forth
Dedicate: DEDICATE — devote yourself, like a DEDICATION page
Deduce: DEDUCE — reason it out like a DEtective, infer
Deem: DEEM — you 'deem it' so, consider it that way
Deficient: DEFICIENT has a DEFICIT — not enough
Definite: DEFINITE is DEFINED clearly, no doubt
Degrade: DEGRADE lowers the GRADE, makes worse
Delegate: DELEGATE hands the task to a representative
Dense: DENSE — packed tight, or too thick to understand
Depict: DEPICT paints a PICTure of it
Deploy: DEPLOY — unfold and position, like troops in the field
Deprive: DEPRIVE takes away — you're 'de-PRIVED' of it

Deter: DETER discourages — a DETERrent stops you

Deviate: DEVIATE — the DEVIous path departs from the norm
Device: A DEVICE is devised for a purpose
Devote: DEVOTE gives all — like a DEVOTED follower
Dilemma: A DILEMMA has two horns — both choices are bad
Dimension: DIMENSION is a measurable extent, like length
Diminish: DIMINISH — it grows DIM and smaller, reduced

Discrete: DISCRETE — each one separate and distinct
Display: DISPLAY shows it out on show
Dispose: DISPOSE — get rid of, or arrange in POSition
Disrupt: DISRUPT breaks up — it interRUPTs and disturbs
Dissolve: DISSOLVE — SOLVE it into liquid until it disappears
Distort: DISTORT twists out of shape — like a TORTured shape
Distribute: DISTRIBUTE gives out shares to each
Divert: DIVERT turns aside — a diVERSion changes course
Document: A DOCUMENT records it in writing
Domain: A DOMAIN is your MAIN area of control
Dominance: DOMINANCE — the DOMINant one holds power
Draft: A DRAFT is a rough first version
Dramatic: DRAMATIC — sudden and striking like a DRAMA
Duration: DURATION — how long it enDUREs in time
Dynamic: DYNAMIC — full of energy, like DYNAMite

Elicit: ELICIT draws out a response — don't confuse with illicit
Eliminate: ELIMINATE — remove completely, wipe it out
Emerge: EMERGE comes out — like surfacing from underwater
Empirical: EMPIRICAL is based on EXPERIment, not theory
Enhance: ENHANCE raises quality — makes it better
Entity: An ENTITY has its own existENCE, a separate thing
Erode: ERODE wears away — water eRODEs the rock over time

Expose: EXPOSE reveals — takes it out of hiding

Extract: EXTRACT pulls out — a dentist exTRACTs a tooth
Finite: FINITE has an end — the FINISH line, a limit
Fluctuate: FLUCTUATE rises and falls, like a FLUID level
Format: FORMAT — the FORM and layout of something
Foundation: A FOUNDATION is the base you FOUND everything on
Framework: A FRAMEWORK is the FRAME holding the system
Fundamental: FUNDAMENTAL is the essential base — the FUNDament
Generate: GENERATE creates — a GENERAtor produces power
Hierarchy: HIERARCHY ranks high to low, tiered levels
Highlight: HIGHLIGHT — shine a HIGH LIGHT to emphasize
Hypothesis: A HYPOTHESIS is a starting guess to test
Ignore: IGNORE — you're 'ignore-ant' of it, take no notice
Illuminate: ILLUMINATE lights up — a LUMINous glow that clarifies
Immigrate: IMMIGRATE — migrate INTO a new country to live
Implicate: IMPLICATE shows involvement — you're IMPLIed in it
Imply: IMPLY suggests without saying — read between lines
Import: IMPORT brings IN through the PORT
Incident: An INCIDENT is an event that happens
Incline: INCLINE leans toward — a slope you're inclined to climb
Induce: INDUCE brings about — you 'in-DUCE', lead it to happen
Inherent: INHERENT is built-in — a natural, permanent quality
Initial: INITIAL is the first — your INITIALs start your name
Initiate: INITIATE begins — the INITIAL start of a process
Injure: INJURE harms — an INJURY causes damage
Innovate: INNOVATE brings NEW — 'in-NOVA', a new star of ideas
Input: INPUT is what you PUT IN
Insist: INSIST demands firmly — you SIT and won't move
Instinct: INSTINCT is inborn — a gut feeling you're born with
Integral: INTEGRAL is essential — an INTEGER makes the whole complete
Interact: INTERACT — ACT between each other, mutual effect
Intermediate: INTERMEDIATE is in the MIDdle, between two
Intervene: INTERVENE comes between — steps IN to alter it
Intimate: INTIMATE is closely personal, INTO the heart
Introduce: INTRODUCE brings in — 'intro-DUCE', lead it in first
Investigate: INVESTIGATE inquires — a detective VESTs in finding facts
Isolate: ISOLATE sets apart — alone on an ISLe
Justify: JUSTIFY proves right — shows it's JUST and fair
Label: A LABEL tags and categorizes
Labor: LABOR is hard work — physical or mental effort
Lack: LACK — you're missing it, not enough
Launch: LAUNCH sets in motion — like launching a rocket
Legislation: LEGISLATION is the LEGAL laws collectively
Liberal: LIBERAL is open and generous — free like LIBERty
Link: A LINK connects two things, like a chain link
Maintain: MAINTAIN keeps it going — MAIN-tains the condition
Manipulate: MANIPULATE handles skillfully — with the hands, 'MANus'
Manner: MANNER is the way it's done
Match: MATCH pairs equally — two that fit
Matter: MATTER — the subject at hand, or physical substance
Mature: MATURE is fully grown and wise
Measure: MEASURE finds the size, or a step you take
Mediate: MEDIATE — the MEDIAtor stands in the middle to settle
Merge: MERGE combines into one — lanes MERGE together
Method: A METHOD is a set procedure to follow
Migrate: MIGRATE moves — birds miGRATE to a new place
Minor: MINOR is smaller — a MINE-or matter, lesser
Minute: MINUTE (my-NEWT) means tiny — small as a minute detail
Mislead: MISLEAD gives a wrong LEAD, a false idea
Motive: A MOTIVE MOVES you to act — your reason
Mutual: MUTUAL — shared both ways, like a mutual friend
Narrow: NARROW is thin — an ARROW is narrow and slim
Negative: NEGATIVE denies — says NO, or is harmful
Neutral: NEUTRAL takes no side — like a car in neutral gear
Nevertheless: NEVERTHELESS — 'never the less', in spite of that
Normal: NORMAL fits the NORM, usual and standard
Notion: A NOTION is an idea you've noted in the mind
Objective: OBJECTIVE — a goal, or fact-based without feelings
Obtain: OBTAIN gets it — you 'ob-TAIN', secure it
Obvious: OBVIOUS is clear — it's right in the way, you see it
Occupy: OCCUPY fills the space — takes up room or time
Offer: OFFER presents it for you to take or leave
Official: OFFICIAL comes from the OFFICE, with authority
Omit: OMIT leaves out — 'oh, missed it', excluded
Opinion: An OPINION is your personal view
Opponent: An OPPONENT stands OPPosite, against you
Opportunity: OPPORTUNITY — a lucky door OPens, favorable time
Oppose: OPPOSE resists — stand OPPosite and disapprove
Option: An OPTION is a choice you may OPT for
Orient: ORIENT aligns you — face the ORIENT, find your way
Origin: ORIGIN is the start — the ORIGINal point
Outcome: OUTCOME is what comes OUT, the result
Outline: An OUTLINE draws the outer LINE, main features
Output: OUTPUT is what's PUT OUT, the result produced
Overcome: OVERCOME — you 'come OVER' the problem, beat it
Overlap: OVERLAP — one part laps OVER another
Overlook: OVERLOOK — look OVER and miss it, or a view from above
Overseas: OVERSEAS — OVER the SEAS, in a foreign land
Overwhelm: OVERWHELM buries you — a WHELMing wave overpowers

Particular: PARTICULAR points to one PART, specific
Partner: A PARTNER takes PART with you
Passive: PASSIVE just accepts — sits and PASSes, no reaction
Percent: PERCENT — 'per hundred', one part in a CENTury of parts
Period: A PERIOD is a portion of time
Permit: PERMIT allows — you 'per-MIT', let it pass
Perspective: PERSPECTIVE is your point of view — how you inSPECT it
